Name: FIDO2 Attestation is not enforced
Description:
This indicator checks if FIDO2 security key attestation is enforced in Entra ID.

Required permissions

  • Policy.Read.All
LikelihoodOfCompromise:
FIDO2 security key attestation is a mechanism for providing assurance during key registration that a key is a specific make and model of key, using a cryptographically signed key pair applied to the device during manufacturing, which can then be verified against the FIDO Alliance Metadata service, so that organizations can ensure that only FIDO certified keys are registered. Further, attestation acts as a layer of protection during key registration, ensuring that a threat actor cannot intercept a registration.

If an organization disables enforcement, there is a lower level of assurance about the origination and security of the key being registered.

References

Enable passkeys for your organization - Microsoft Entra ID | Microsoft Learn
Remediation:
Enable Enforce attestation in Entra ID for FIDO2 security key settings.

To enable, open the Microsoft Entra admin center (direct link), expand Protection and select Authentication methods.

In the blade on the right, select Policies, and then under Method select FIDO2 security key.

In the new blade that opens, select Configure from the top menu. Toggle Enforce attestation to yes.

Select Save at the bottom of the blade.

Organizations must test changes to FIDO2 security key settings as this change is user impacting to enrollments, and ensure that changes are communicated out as necessary
